steps.title: Identify what the task requires
steps.content: The student wants to contrast "Poor Miner's Farewell" with "Bring Him Back Home." Contrast means showing how the two differ — the correct choice must mention both songs and highlight a meaningful difference between them, not a similarity or a description of only one.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — The songs "Poor Miner's Farewell" and "Bring Him Back Home" both raised awareness about human rights violations.: The word both signals similarity, not contrast. This violates the task requirement. ✗
  • Choice B — While both are protest songs, "Poor Miner's Farewell" is about coal miners in Kentucky, whereas "Bring Him Back Home" is about the anti-apartheid leader Nelson Mandela.: The structure "while both… whereas…" acknowledges a shared category while drawing a clear difference in subject matter between the two songs. Both songs are named and contrasted. ✓
  • Choice C — Hugh Masekela's song "Bring Him Back Home," released in 1987, called on the South African government to free Nelson Mandela.: Only one song is described; "Poor Miner's Farewell" is not mentioned at all, so no contrast is possible. ✗
  • Choice D — Released in 1932 by Aunt Molly Jackson, the song "Poor Miner's Farewell" was a protest against the unlivable wages and dangerous working conditions faced by Kentucky coal miners.: Only one song is described; "Bring Him Back Home" is not mentioned at all, so no contrast is possible. ✗
That is the correct answer.
